records.ety: [Gr. <?/ to be like the liver, to be liver-colored, fr. <?/, <?/, the liver: cf. E. hepatite, and (for sense 2) F. hpatiser.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Hepatized (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Hepatizing (?).]

defs: To impregnate with sulphureted hydrogen gas, formerly called hepatic gas.
quotes: On the right . . . were two wells of hepatized water.

defs: To gorge with effused matter, as the lungs.
